summary: date-fns-lite postinstall exfiltrates host, cloud IMDS, and Kubernetes credentials to hardcoded IP
details: date-fns-lite@1.0.3 declares a postinstall hook (postinstall.js) that runs automatically on `npm install`. The script collects installer-side identifiers (os.hostname(), username, network interfaces, selected environment variables, /etc/hostname, /etc/hosts, /proc/self/cgroup) and reads the Kubernetes service account token at /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token. It additionally probes cloud instance metadata services — AWS IMDSv2 (iam/security-credentials), GCP metadata.google.internal (service-accounts/default/token with Metadata-Flavor:Google), Aliyun 100.100.100.200 (ram/security-credentials), Tencent metadata.tencentyun.com (cam/security-credentials), and Huawei OpenStack metadata — to harvest short-lived cloud IAM credentials. The combined report is POSTed to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://115.190.124.243:9082/report over plain HTTP. The package name impersonates the popular `date-fns` library, and its advertised date-formatting API is a thin stub — the postinstall harvest is the real payload. Installing this package on any developer workstation, CI runner, or pod leaks host identity, cloud IAM credentials, and Kubernetes service account tokens to an attacker, enabling lateral movement into the installer's cloud account and cluster.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
